summary: Preinstall hook exfiltrates host identity and /etc/passwd to oastify.com
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle script that runs curl against an attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator subdomain (78dngdm3dhrrj8zgfm4es9m8bzhq5jt8.oastify.com) over plain HTTP. The request URL embeds the output of whoami, pwd, hostname, ls of the parent directory, and the contents of /etc/passwd as query parameters. Any developer or CI system running `npm install pocteszep` automatically transmits user identity, host metadata, working-directory contents, and a sensitive system file to the attacker. The package's own description string acknowledges it is a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept, and the preinstall command is its sole payload — there is no library functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
